title: Tubella barbelifera

The classification of Tubella barbelifera places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Coelogyneae. This plant is an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants for support, and is typically found in shaded, humid environments. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification within the Orchidaceae family.

The "Wild Potato" is rich in health benefits, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tubera barbae include phenolic compounds, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
